items.headline: Wemby Drops 41 & 24 as Spurs Stun Thunder in Double-OT WCF Thriller!
items.summary: Victor Wembanyama delivered a monumental 41 points and 24 rebounds, along with 3 blocks, as the San Antonio Spurs edged out the Oklahoma City Thunder 122-115 in a double-overtime Game 1 of the Western Conference Finals. The Thunder suffered their first playoff loss of the season, snapping a nine-game winning streak. Shai Gilgeous-Alexander received his second consecutive MVP trophy before the game, but his 24 points and 12 assists weren't enough. Rookie Dylan Harper also made history for the Spurs with a sensational performance.

items.headline: Eastern Conference Finals Kick Off Tonight: Knicks Host Cavaliers in Game 1
items.summary: The highly anticipated Eastern Conference Finals between the Cleveland Cavaliers and the New York Knicks is set to begin tonight at Madison Square Garden. The Knicks enter with momentum after sweeping the 76ers, while the Cavaliers battled through seven games against the Pistons. OG Anunoby (NYK) is probable to play despite a hamstring issue, while Larry Nance Jr. (CLE) is questionable due to illness.

items.headline: LeBron James, 41, Becomes Unrestricted Free Agent, Drawing League-Wide Contender Interest
items.summary: After completing his 23rd NBA season, 41-year-old LeBron James is now an unrestricted free agent, with reports indicating that every championship-contending team in the league is expressing interest in acquiring his services. His future decision could significantly impact the offseason landscape.

items.headline: WCF Game 1: Spurs Take Down Thunder in Double OT Thriller
items.summary: The San Antonio Spurs delivered a stunning upset in Game 1 of the Western Conference Finals, defeating the Oklahoma City Thunder in a double-overtime epic.

items.headline: ECF Game 1: Cavaliers at Knicks
items.summary: The Eastern Conference Finals begin tonight as the Cleveland Cavaliers visit the New York Knicks at Madison Square Garden.

items.headline: Wemby's Historic WCF Debut
items.statContext: Became youngest player with 40+ points & 20+ rebounds in a playoff game in Game 1 vs OKC.

items.headline: Rookie Dylan Harper Shines
items.statContext: Set a Spurs franchise playoff record for steals and scored the most points by a rookie in a Conference Finals game since 1981 in Game 1 vs OKC.

items.headline: Caruso's Scoring Surge
items.statContext: Delivered a playoff career-high off the bench in Game 1 vs SAS.

items.headline: Deni Avdija's Season Performance
items.statContext: The Israeli forward completed a 66-game season for the Portland Trail Blazers. He recently showcased a 30-point, 10-rebound, 5-assist game earlier in the playoffs.

items.headline: MVP Shai Gilgeous-Alexander
items.statContext: Received his second consecutive MVP trophy before Game 1 of the WCF.

items.headline: De'Aaron Fox Day-to-Day with Ankle Soreness, Optimistic for Game 2
items.summary: Spurs All-Star guard De'Aaron Fox missed Game 1 of the Western Conference Finals due to right ankle soreness but is reportedly optimistic about returning for Game 2 against the Thunder on Wednesday.

items.headline: Jalen Williams Returns to Action for Thunder
items.summary: Oklahoma City Thunder forward Jalen Williams made his return in Game 1 of the Western Conference Finals, scoring 26 points after missing six games due to a left hamstring strain.

items.headline: Offseason Trade Rumors Swirl Around Star Players
items.summary: As teams conclude their seasons, the NBA trade rumor mill is heating up. Speculation is rampant around superstars like Giannis Antetokounmpo, Donovan Mitchell, and Kawhi Leonard as potential trade candidates, with teams like the Pistons reportedly eyeing Leonard and Lauri Markkanen.

items.headline: Injury Report for Eastern Conference Finals Game 1
items.summary: New York Knicks forward OG Anunoby is listed as probable for tonight's Eastern Conference Finals Game 1 against the Cavaliers due to a hamstring injury. Cleveland's Larry Nance Jr. is questionable for the contest with an illness.
